libraryPreparationPreflight.agent_guidance: Do not add extra Maven dependencies or Docker setup. If tests exercise OGNL internals, accessibility handlers, constructors, method/property access on JDK classes, or private/member-access behavior, configure the test JVM with `--add-opens=java.base/java.lang=ALL-UNNAMED` and likely `--add-opens=java.base/java.util=ALL-UNNAMED`, matching upstream OGNL's JDK 17+ test profile. Avoid relying on unguarded `System.setSecurityManager` on JDK 25; if sandbox behavior is covered, set/restore `ognl.security.manager` inside the test and tolerate dynamic SecurityManager installation being unsupported.

input_evidence.name: issue
input_evidence.summary: #7430 [Automation] java run fails for ognl:ognl:3.3.5; label=fails-java-run; body_chars=8015

risks: Basic parse/get/set OGNL usage may pass without module opens, but coverage of the reflection and accessibility paths that matter for reachability metadata can fail or be skipped without them.
SecurityManager-related OGNL paths are fragile on modern JDKs and should be guarded rather than treated as mandatory JVM setup.
libraryPreparationPreflight.summary: OGNL itself needs no external service and its only runtime dependency, Javassist, is already transitive, but upstream OGNL 3.3.5 configures JDK 17+ test JVM module opens; generated tests should preserve those JVM args if they exercise OGNL reflection/accessibility internals.
